title: Review FSM encoding and safety
description: Check a state machine for a defined reset state, no unreachable/illegal states, and safe handling of undefined inputs.
body: Steps: 1. Extract the state set and transition table. 2. Confirm a defined reset/initial state and that every state is reachable. 3. Add a default/others branch routing illegal states back to a safe state. 4. Choose encoding (binary vs one-hot) per timing/area needs and recovery requirements. 5. Add assertions that the FSM never enters an undefined state. Rollback point: before re-encoding states. Validation: simulation/assertions show no illegal-state entry and recovery from injected upsets; a one-hot FSM with no illegal-state recovery is unsafe in noisy environments.
